title: Flaky Citrusy Argentinian Chicken Empanadas
description: This recipe brings together the flaky texture of Argentinian empanadas, the brightness of citrus, and the warmth of chilli powder. Perfect for a quick meal prep solution.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Add cold butter and use a pastry blender to cut it into small pieces until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add yuzu juice and mix until the dough starts to come together. Gradually add water, mixing until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes pliable and easy to handle.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each one into a ball.
Use a rolling pin to flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of yuzu-cured chicken in the center of each circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with egg wash and fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before baking.
Bake the empanadas for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before serving.
